question: Describe the two primary methods for obtaining initial estimates for the autoregressive (p) and moving average (q) orders in an ARMA model, and state one key consideration for each.
expectedPoints: The Autocorrelation Function (ACF) is used to identify potential MA(q) order, as its theoretical pattern cuts off after lag q.
The Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF) is used to identify potential AR(p) order, as its theoretical pattern cuts off after lag p.
A key consideration is that these are theoretical patterns; in practice, sample ACF/PACF plots can be messy and may not show a clear cutoff, requiring judgement.

question: Explain why obtaining good initial estimates is a crucial step in the overall model-building process for ARIMA models.
expectedPoints: Good initial estimates provide a starting point close to the true parameter values for the subsequent iterative optimization algorithm.
This improves the chances of the algorithm converging to the global optimum (true parameter values) rather than a local optimum or failing to converge.
It makes the estimation process computationally more efficient and faster by reducing the number of iterations required.

question: Outline a step-by-step procedure for obtaining initial parameter estimates for a non-seasonal ARIMA(p,d,q) model. Your answer should cover the entire process from a raw time series through to having initial values for φ and θ parameters, including how the initial values for the mean are handled.
expectedPoints: First, ensure the series is stationary by applying differencing d times to obtain the series W_t = ∇^d Y_t.
Analyze the ACF and PACF of the stationary series W_t to tentatively identify the orders p (from the PACF) and q (from the ACF).

If the mean of W_t is significantly different from zero (μ_w ≠ 0), estimate it using the sample mean (x̄_w) and center the series to create V_t = W_t - x̄_w. For an ARMA(p,q) model with no constant term, this mean is used as the initial estimate.

Use the Yule-Walker equations for an AR(p) model or the method of moments for an MA(q) model to calculate initial estimates for the φ (AR) and θ (MA) parameters from the sample autocovariances of V_t.
